paper.title: Runaway BN supergiant star HD 93840: Progenitor of an imminent core-collapse supernova above the Galactic plane

extraction.summary: The paper presents a detailed analysis of HD~93840 as a runaway star ejected from the Galactic disk. While it mentions hyper-runaway and hypervelocity stars in the introduction as a broader context, HD~93840 itself is never presented as possibly unbound from the Milky Way. Its kinematics are modelled within the Galactic potential and it is bound to the Galaxy. Therefore, no HVS/unbound candidates are included.

candidate_groups_considered.description: The single runaway star HD~93840 (BN1 Ib) studied in this paper.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper treats HD~93840 as a runaway star ejected from the Galactic disk by a supernova explosion, with its orbit computed within the Galactic potential. It is never presented as possibly unbound from the Milky Way. The introduction explicitly distinguishes runaway stars from hyper-runaway and hypervelocity stars, placing HD~93840 in the runaway category.

source_refs.context: Abstract describing HD~93840 as a runaway star ejected by a supernova explosion.
Introduction contrasting runaway stars with hyper-runaway and hypervelocity stars.
Discussion explicitly stating HD~93840 is a runaway star originating from the Carina-Sagittarius spiral arm, with orbit traced in the Galactic potential.
